text.heading: False rejoicing in Yobe
text.p: SIR: Kindly permit me a space in your widely read newspaper to offer an advice to Ali Sheriff, Mamman Ali and Bukar Abba Ibrahim for the so-called victory lecture held at Warsala near Damaturu over a judgment made by the Supreme Court in respect of the Yobe State gubernatorial tussle.

The five men-panel of the apex court presided over by Justice Katsina Alu told Sen. Albishir that "he put the apex court at conflict with the lower Courts, as the appeal to the supreme court was made on the case whose decision was yet to be taken by the lower court entertaining it. Therefore, it is the opinion of the Supreme Court that Sen. Albishir should go back to the Court of Appeal Jos, and judgment must have been made before the Apex Court shall entertain the case.

With the above statement issued by the highly respected Supreme Court Judge, is it therefore proper that the two serving Governors and a Distinguish Senator of the Federal Republic of Nigeria to go ahead and deceive the electorate that the Yobe Guber tussle is over, and therefore, the door is open for Sen. Albishir to join and support the Mamman Aliled government, otherwise if he fails to do so within a shortest possible time they will continue to deal with him brutally and in a barbaric way until he is escorted to his grave yard?

Usually, an obedient servant of God always prays for long life and prosperity to his parents in order to guide him to the straight path and to keep him away from wrong doings. Among the above mentioned politicians, Senator Mamman Ali and Senator Ali Sheriff are assumed to be the beneficiaries of this age-long cherished prayer.
In this case the pertinent question to ask is: are they at all seeking their parents’ advice or are their parents at all giving them proper advice?
Finally may I use this opportunity and advice Ali Sheriff and co to revisit their inhuman statement and seek forgiveness from Almighty Creator and good people Yobe State.
